# UC-22.2.38 · NIS2 Industrial Control System Patching and Change Evidence

## Description

Compares the latest firmware string and last engineering change timestamp observed in OT vendor telemetry against an approved baseline table to find PLCs or RTUs that drifted from policy or never recorded a change after the last CAB approval epoch.

## Value

Plant managers can hand an inspector a Splunk report that ties patch windows to actual controller versions without walking the shop floor with USB sticks, reducing downtime disputes when a CVE fix was scheduled but not propagated.

## Implementation

(1) Route Rockwell/Siemens/Schneider engineering tool exports or agent logs to `index=ot_changes` through HEC with TLS. (2) Maintain `nis2_ot_patch_policy.csv` from monthly OT CAB minutes including `last_approved_change_epoch`. (3) Pull `snow:change_request` (1928) for contextual CHG numbers — join in a second panel. (4) Schedule search weekly due to low event rates. (5) Store evidence **7 years** in `audit_evidence` if mandated.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Dedicated HEC token on indexers accepting OT VLAN sources; restrict network ACL to engineering jump hosts only.
• Universal Forwarder **not** always possible on PLCs — prefer vendor gateway or scheduled CSV push via scripted input.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) with read-only `change_request` role.
• Governance: OT CAB publishes CSV updates into `lookups/` through git-controlled pipeline with checksum validation.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Create HEC token under **Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector** named `ot_patch_hec`, index=`ot_changes`, sourcetype=`ot:vendor:patch`.
On engineering workstation, use vendor SDK or PowerShell to POST JSON `{"asset_id":"PLC12","firmware_version":"v3.2.1"}` every **15 minutes** after maintenance windows.
Import `nis2_ot_patch_policy.csv` columns: asset_id, required_fw_version, last_approved_change_epoch (unix seconds).
Enable ServiceNow change_request pull every **600 seconds** filtered to `u_ot_relevant=true` custom flag.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run weekly (`cron 15 5 * * 1`) with `earliest=-90d@d`. Save `UC-22.2.38_ot_patch_drift`.
Alert when `fw_compliant=0` for assets tagged `tier=0` in lookup extension column.
Optional join: `| join asset_id [ search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request | stats latest(start_date) as chg_start by cmdb_ci | rename cmdb_ci as asset_id ]`.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick asset recently patched in vendor tool; confirm `firmware_version` field matches Splunk `observed_fw`.
Simulate CSV bump: raise `required_fw_version` — search should flag row until telemetry catches up.
Validate `last_approved_change_epoch` aligns with ServiceNow `start_date` converted to epoch — differences indicate CAB data defect not Splunk defect.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard split by plant: filter `asset_id` prefix. Integrate with SOAR playbook asking engineer attach vendor PDF certificate.
Archive weekly PDF to `index=audit_evidence` with `_meta` tagging `clause=Art.21(2)(e)`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No ot_changes events** — HEC token disabled post cluster restart; re-enable via distsearch peer baseline.
• **lookup missing asset** — default `required_fw_version` null skips compare; enforce completeness with splunk ingest validation.
• **last_change always equals now** — duplicate events each poll; dedup `asset_id` using `stats latest(_time)` already — check `_raw` uniqueness.
• **change_stale stuck at 1** — telemetry timestamp never updates because device offline — add liveness panel.
• **string firmware compare too strict** — switch to `if(like(observed_fw,required_fw_version+"%"),1,0)` pattern for build numbers.

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_changes earliest=-90d
| stats latest(firmware_version) as observed_fw latest(_time) as last_change by asset_id
| lookup nis2_ot_patch_policy.csv asset_id OUTPUT required_fw_version last_approved_change_epoch
| eval fw_compliant=if(observed_fw=required_fw_version,1,0)
| eval change_stale=if(last_change < last_approved_change_epoch,1,0)
| where fw_compliant=0 OR change_stale=1
| table asset_id, observed_fw, required_fw_version, last_change, change_stale
```

## Visualization

Table of non-compliant asset_id, timeline of last_change, bar chart of stale changes by plant site.
